next_steps: Check serum TSH, free T4, and total T3 levels
Thyroid ultrasound to evaluate the hypoattenuating nodule and vascularity
Erythrocyte sedimentation rate (ESR) and C-reactive protein (CRP) trend monitoring
Radioactive iodine uptake (RAIU) scan if TSH is suppressed
Consider fine-needle aspiration (FNA) of the thyroid nodule if ultrasound appearance is suspicious
Discontinue antibiotics if bacterial cultures remain negative and thyroid pathology is confirmed
